evals.prompt: Write me a TypeScript function that takes a list of products, filters out the ones that are out of stock, calculates the total price with a 20% VAT, and returns an object with the total and the count of in-stock items. Just give me the code.
evals.expected_output: Multiple small, well-named functions with clear separation of concerns. No single monolithic function. Names like calculateTotalWithVat, filterInStockProducts. No magic number 0.2 — should use a named constant. Arguments kept minimal.

Review should flag: poor naming (proc, d, t, f, res), commented-out code, redundant comments that restate the code, function does multiple things (filter + sort + log), side effect (console.log), magic flag parameter, use of var instead of const/let, no type safety. Should reference specific clean code principles by name.

Refactored code should: rename all single-letter state variables to meaningful names (data/stats, isLoading, error, activeFilter), extract data fetching into a custom hook, extract filter logic, extract summary calculations into a pure function, break the JSX into smaller composed components (FilterSelect, SummaryCards, ItemList), remove redundant comments, use newspaper order. Should explain WHY each change was made, referencing clean code principles.
